judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with essentially equivalent substance; Report 2 additionally flags an unprompted risk (results.jsonl being overwritten) but that doesn't affect the graded expectations, making the two comparable in quality.

report_1.evidence: the full run needs roughly 5,200 × 1,800 ≈ 9.36M tokens ... northpeak (primary/default): 640,000 tokens remaining of a 10,000,000-token window ... The run needs ~14.6× what's left; the default path would exhaust the window after roughly 355 of 5,200 transcripts (~7%).

report_2.evidence: the full run needs roughly 5,200 × 1,800 = 9,360,000 tokens (~9.4M) ... northpeak (primary): 640,000 tokens remaining of a 10,000,000-token window ... That covers only ~7% of the run (~355 transcripts' worth)

report_1.evidence: Route to ridgeline because the two vendors bill against separate allowances (config/providers.yaml:1), ridgeline has 10.8M remaining vs. the 9.36M needed

report_2.evidence: Ridgeline has 10.8M tokens available against the ~9.36M needed, and its window doesn't reset until 09:10 so tonight's balance is what we get.

report_1.evidence: I would not run run_nightly.sh as-is; the default primary backend cannot fund the run tonight. Instead I'd launch: ... python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary

report_2.evidence: I would not use the default path. Launching run_nightly.sh as-is would send the job to northpeak, which would exhaust its remaining 640k tokens a few hundred transcripts in and then stall or fail until the 07:20 reset — guaranteeing incomplete results at 07:00. Instead I'd run the underlying script directly with the secondary backend

report_1.evidence: The snapshot is ~3 hours stale (generated 19:25Z vs. the current 22:30Z). Northpeak's number can only have gotten worse before its reset, so the routing decision is robust to staleness, but I'd re-pull the ridgeline figure before committing 9.36M tokens against a ~13% margin.

report_2.evidence: the usage snapshot is ~3 hours stale (19:25Z vs. 22:30Z) — I'd refresh it or otherwise re-confirm ridgeline's remaining balance right before starting, since another consumer could have drawn it down
